idea.hook: How far is the nearest star, *REALLY*? This scale will blow your mind!
facts: If our Sun was shrunk to the size of a grapefruit (10 cm diameter)...
...Earth would be a tiny grain of sand (0.1 cm) orbiting 10 meters away.
And Proxima Centauri, our closest star, on this exact scale?
idea.payoff: It would be more than 2,800 KILOMETERS away! That's like the distance from London to Athens. Space is unimaginably vast.
idea.title: 🤯🤯🤯 The Nearest Star is HOW FAR?! #SpaceScale

scenes.text: How far is the nearest star, *REALLY*?
scenes.visual: Deep space background with swirling galaxies. Text appears prominently with a subtle glow.

scenes.text: If our SUN was a grapefruit (10cm)...
scenes.visual: A photorealistic grapefruit appears in the center, glowing softly like a miniature sun. Dark space background.

scenes.text: ...EARTH would be a grain of sand (0.1cm) 10m away.
scenes.visual: The grapefruit (Sun) shrinks and moves to the bottom-left corner. A tiny grain of sand (Earth) appears far to the right, with a faint dashed line indicating 10m distance. Dark space background.

scenes.text: Proxima Centauri (nearest star) on THIS scale?
scenes.visual: The grapefruit and sand fade out or shrink further to obscurity. A large, glowing question mark appears in the center of the screen, against a dark, empty space.

scenes.text:
It's 2,800+ KILOMETERS AWAY!
(London to Athens)
SPACE IS UNIMAGINABLY VAST.
scenes.visual: A world map appears, highlighting London and Athens with a dashed line connecting them. This visual zooms out, then fades to a deep, dark void of space with faint, distant stars.
